description_long: This object is a metallic, cylindrical electric drill with a robust, industrial design. The main body is composed of a silver-gray metal with a ribbed, black grip section in the center, likely for improved handling. At one end, there is a wider, rounded metallic cap, while the opposite end features a more angular, mechanical housing with subtle detailing. A copper-colored ring separates the grip from the cap, adding a touch of contrast to the otherwise monochrome palette. The object lacks a visible drill bit, and there are no clear signs of a power cord or battery pack. The surface appears hard and slightly worn, suggesting frequent use in urban or workshop environments.
description: A metallic, cylindrical electric drill with a ribbed black grip, silver-gray body, and copper accent ring. No drill bit or power source is visible.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length, ribbed grip, copper ring, and both end caps clearly.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the wide, rounded metallic cap and the concentric structure of the drill.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with the ribbed grip and mechanical housing visible.
description_view_3: End-on view from the angular housing side, showing the mechanical details and lack of a drill bit.
